207	When your chapter's proofs rely on a definition or lemma that **Mathlib already provides** (and the project will not re-prove), it is good practice — not just a bare `\uses{}` to nowhere — to make that dependency a first-class blueprint block: a **Mathlib dependency anchor**. Doing so:

\begin{lemma}[The slice structure-sheaf ring map $\psi_r$]
  \label{lem:slice_structureSheaf_hom}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sliceStructureSheafHom}
  % NOTE: build target. The Lean declaration does not exist yet.
  Let \(\varphi : X \cong Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes, \(W \subseteq X\) an open and
  \(V = \varphi.\mathrm{inv}^{-1}\,W \subseteq Y\) its preimage. There is a morphism of sheaves of
  rings \(\psi_r\) over the slice (over-category) picture relating the structure sheaf of \(W\) to that
  of \(V\), obtained by restricting the structure-sheaf morphism \(\varphi.\mathrm{hom}\) induces
  between \(\mathcal{O}_X\) and \(\mathcal{O}_Y\) (its associated sheaf-of-rings morphism) to the
  over-category over \(W\). This single cross-ring slice structure-sheaf ring map is the datum from
  which the pullback functor of Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_slice_pullback_iso} is built.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  The morphism \(\varphi.\mathrm{hom}\) carries a canonical map of structure sheaves; transport it to
  the over-category over \(W\) using the Beck--Chevalley identity that post-composition with a functor
  commutes with the forgetful functor out of the slice (the equality
  \(\operatorname{Over.post} F \circ \operatorname{Over.forget} = \operatorname{Over.forget} \circ F\)
  holds definitionally). The only non-definitional adjustment is the unit-isomorphism correction built
  into the over-category transport equivalence (the \(\operatorname{Over.map}\) along the unit
  isomorphism). The result is the slice structure-sheaf ring map \(\psi_r\) over \(V\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[The pullback-unit comparison, an iso for a final functor]
  \label{lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_mathlib}
  \lean{SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit, SheafOfModules.instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  In the setting of Lemma~\ref{lem:sheafOfModules_pullback_mathlib} there is a canonical comparison
  morphism \(\operatorname{pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\varphi :
  (\operatorname{pullback}\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(\operatorname{unit} S) \to \operatorname{unit} R\)
  between the pullback of the unit (free rank-one) module and the unit module of the target. When the
  underlying functor \(F\) is final (\(F.\operatorname{Final}\)) this comparison is an isomorphism.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Pullback along $\psi_r$ realizes the per-slice pushforward]
  \label{lem:pushforward_slice_pullback_iso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardSlicePullbackIso}
  % NOTE: build target. The Lean declaration does not exist yet.
  \uses{lem:slice_structureSheaf_hom, lem:sheafOfModules_pullback_mathlib,
    lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_mathlib}
  Let \(\varphi : X \cong Y\), \(U_i \subseteq X\), \(V_i = \varphi.\mathrm{inv}^{-1}\,U_i\), let
  \(\psi_r\) be the slice structure-sheaf ring map of Lemma~\ref{lem:slice_structureSheaf_hom}, and write
  \(\Phi = \operatorname{pushforwardEquivOfIso}\varphi\). For every \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(H\) the
  pullback functor \(\operatorname{pullback}\psi_r : \operatorname{SheafOfModules}\mathcal{O}_{U_i} \to
  \operatorname{SheafOfModules}\mathcal{O}_{V_i}\) applied directly to the slice
  \(H.\operatorname{over} U_i\) (which already lies in its domain) is isomorphic to the restricted
  pushforward:
  \[
    \bigl(\operatorname{pullback}\psi_r\bigr).\mathrm{obj}\bigl(H.\operatorname{over} U_i\bigr)
      \;\cong\;
    \bigl(\Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, H\bigr).\operatorname{over} V_i .
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:slice_structureSheaf_hom, lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_mathlib}
  Assemble the isomorphism from two pieces. First, the pullback-unit comparison
  \(\operatorname{pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\psi_r\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_mathlib}) is an
  isomorphism because the opens-equivalence functor \(F\) is final; it identifies
  \((\operatorname{pullback}\psi_r).\mathrm{obj}\) of the slice with the pushforward read over the image
  site. Second, the opens-equivalence object identity \(F.\mathrm{obj}\, U_i = V_i\) holds by
  \(\operatorname{rfl}\) on the underlying opens: the image functor sends an open \(W\) to
  \(\varphi.\mathrm{inv}^{-1}\,W\) definitionally, so no \(\operatorname{eqToIso}\) correction along an
  open-set equality is required. Composing the unit isomorphism with this definitional identification of
  the underlying open yields the stated isomorphism.
\end{proof}
